description.value: Factor to convert ADC counts to volts
Enable channel for the acquisition. When the channel is disabled, it doesn't give any data and its self-trigger is off.
Set gain of a Variable Gain Amplifier
Get a 32 bit word representing the status of the channel
A constant DC offset (controlled by a 16 bit DAC) is added to the analog input in order to adjust the position of the signal baseline.
Dimensionless value to convert ADC counts to volts
Determine if the channel partecipate to ITLA or ITLB.
Offset of the input signal.
For test purposes, it is possible to replace the ADC data with internal data generators.

Arms the digitizer to start an acquisition.
Clear data from memories. Registers content is not affected.
Disarms the acquisition and prevents the digitizer to start a new run.
Trigger OpenData endpoint read
Set a timer that reboots the board after 4 seconds. The communication is lost and must be established again after the reboot. The default firmware is loded.
Delete calibration file and reload it from flash or, if invalid, use default calibration
Resets the board. This command sets all registers to the default value and clear data from memories.
Send a software trigger to the digitizer, provided that the option SwTrg is enabled in the parameter AcqTriggerSource.
Start the acquisition, provided that the option SWcmd is enabled in the parameter StartSource.
Stop the acquisition, provided that the option SWcmd is enabled in the parameter StartSource.
Open FPGA specific data endpoint
Decoded Open DPP
Active endpoint
Raw endpoint
Assigns the direction of quartet LVDS I/Os.
Selects the functionality of a quartet LVDS lines.
Assigns the direction of quartet LVDS I/Os.
Selects the functionality of a quartet LVDS lines.
Assigns the direction of quartet LVDS I/Os.
Selects the functionality of a quartet LVDS lines.
Assigns the direction of quartet LVDS I/Os.
Selects the functionality of a quartet LVDS lines.
Defines the source for the Acquisition Trigger, which is the signal that opens the acquisition window and saves the waveforms in the memory buffers.
Get a 32 bit word representing the acquisition status of the digitizer
Number of bits of the ADCs
Sampling rate of the ADCs
Return true if ErrorFlags is zero.
This parameter defines the source of the Busy Input.
Source of the system clock.
CUP version
When the DACoutMode is set to ChInput, the DAC reproduces the inputs signal received by a selected channel
Mode of the DAC output
Level of DAC output when mode is set to static
Duty cycle of the DCDC converter
When enabled, the Auto Disarm option disarms the acquisition at the stop of run.
Enable clock output on Front Panel for the daisy chain propagation of the clock between multiple boards.
If enabled, events consisting of 2 words are compressed in a single word event.
The calibration can be disabled, mainly when a new calibration has to be calculated.
All these errors can be combined into a Data Error Flag through a masked OR, programmed by this parameter.
All these errors can be combined into a Global Error Flag through a masked OR, programmed by this parameter.
32 bit word with error flags.
e.g. 2730, to indicate all versions of the  XX2730 (VME, VME64X, desktop, SE/DIFF, etc...)
0=VME, 1=VME64X, 2=DT
Firmware version
Frequency of the DCDC converter
Firmware type
IPv4 gateway of ethernet link
Select the signal that is routed to the GPIO, when this is used as output.
Input dynamic range
Single ended or differential (0=SE, 1=DIFF)
Sets the electrical logic level of the LEMO I/Os.
IOut of the DCDC converter
IPv4 address of ethernet link
Gate generator at the output of the ITLs. The combinatorial logic of the self-trigger.
Set the logic of ITLA to combine all the self-triggers of the board according to a specific trigger logic.
Defines the majority level of the Main Logic of the ITLA.
Enable Mask at the input of the ITLA.
Pairs of channels can be combined with an OR or AND before feeding the Main trigger Logic.
Polarity of ITLA.
Gate generator at the output of the ITLs. The combinatorial logic of the self-trigger.
Set the logic of ITLB to combine all the self-triggers of the board according to a specific trigger logic.
Defines the majority level of the Main Logic of the ITLB.
Enable Mask at the input of the ITLB.
Pairs of channels can be combined with an OR or AND before feeding the Main trigger Logic.
Polarity of ITLB.
PUC for DPP firmware
Remaining time to automatic run stop in case of invalid license
License status
Set the status of the LVDS I/O for the quartets that are programmed to be output and Mode = IORegister.
Each LVDS line can be assigned to a combination of the 32 self-triggers, implemented as a masked OR, where the mask is set by this parameter.
Maximum size that can be returned from a single call to GetData from the raw endpoint.
CAEN code (e.g. WV2730XAAAAA)
CAEN model name (e.g. V2730)
IPv4 netmask of ethernet link
Number of channels
OpenData start address
Select OpenData mode: if OpenDataSize is N and OpenDataAddress is A, FIFO reads N times A, RAM reads N registers starting from A
OpenData size to be read, expressed in number of registers
CAEN code of the PB (e.g. WA40BXAAAAAA)
PCB Rev. (Mother Board)
PCB Rev. (Piggy Back)
Stores the clock output delay into the flash memory and makes it permanent.
Allow the start of the acquisition to be delayed with respect to the rising edge of the RUN signal.
Serial Number
Specify the link status of SFP
Specify the presence of SFP
Specify the link protocol of SFP
Speed of the fan 1 (only desktop)
Speed of the fan 2 (only desktop)
Defines the source for the start of run.
This parameter defines the SyncOut mode.
Temperature at the ADC sensor #0
Temperature at the ADC sensor #1 (only for PCB rev 1)
Temperature at the ADC sensor #2 (only for PCB rev 1)
Temperature at the ADC sensor #3 (only for PCB rev 1)
Temperature at the ADC sensor #4 (only for PCB rev 1)
Temperature at the ADC sensor #5 (only for PCB rev 1)
Temperature at the ADC sensor #6 (only for PCB rev 1)
Temperature at the ADC sensor #7
Temperature at the board air entrance in a VME crate (bottom)
Temperature at the board air out of the VME crate (top)
Temperature at the FPGA core
Temperature at the DCDC converter
Temperature at the first ADC
Temperature at the hottest ADC
Temperature at the last ADC
High level of the test pulse.
Low level of the test pulse.
Period of the Test Pulse, a programmable square wave that can be used as an internal periodic trigger or to generate a logic test pulse (TTL or NIM) on the TRGOUT and GPIO outputs.
Width (i.e. the time the signal stays high = 1) of the test pulse in ns.
Select the signal that is routed to the TRGOUT output.
This parameter defines the polarity of the Veto.
This parameter defines the source of the Veto.
Veto width in nanoseconds. When 0, the monostable is disabled and the veto lasts as long as the selected source is active.
VIn of the DCDC converter
Sets the delay of the clock output with respect to the input reference clock.
VOut of the DCDC converter
Input impedance
